metric_value: 133 MB/s shared by all devices on bus
notes: Fundamental bottleneck motivating IBA; PCI-X only offered incremental improvement.

metric_value: 500 MB/s per link minimum scaling to 6 GB/s; 12-wire config reaches 30 Gb/s
notes: 1x/4x/12x lane configurations; each lane at 2.5 Gb/s.

metric_value: 200+ technology sponsors
notes: Steering committee: Compaq Dell HP Intel IBM Microsoft Sun. Sponsors include Cisco Adaptec Lucent Nortel NEC Hitachi Fujitsu-Siemens 3COM.

metric_value: Phased deployment beginning 2003; HCA silicon on system board by late 2003
notes: Aberdeen recommended enterprises begin evaluation in 2002 and move to phased deployment in 2003.

metric_value: [UNVERIFIED]
notes: IBA became dominant HPC interconnect (>70% TOP500 by 2010s) but did not broadly replace PCI-X in general enterprise; became critical for AI/ML training and HPC.

metric_name: Major computer suppliers offering IBA-enabled blades
metric_value: All major suppliers offering IBA-enabled blades by 2003
notes: Aberdeen Group explicit prediction: Compaq Dell IBM Sun to offer IBA blades in 2003.

metric_name: IBA-enabled blade server market — actual outcome
metric_value: Largely validated; HP/IBM/Dell shipped IBA-capable blade systems 2003-2004
notes: Blade + IBA prediction proved correct; became standard for HPC blade racks.

metric_value: Up to three levels of redundancy
notes: Single or dual HCA per server; redundant IBA switch in rack.

metric_value: Three: Host Channel Adapter (HCA) + Target Channel Adapter (TCA) + Switches/Routers
notes: HCA in server; TCA in storage/device; switches within subnet; routers between subnets.

metric_value: Offloads transport from host CPU via InfiniBand processor organizing I/O into packets
notes: Contrasted with TCP/IP where host OS handles all packet processing; RDMA reduces CPU overhead dramatically.

metric_name: RDMA influence on modern networking — actual outcome
metric_value: RDMA concepts from IBA directly influenced RoCE (RDMA over Converged Ethernet) and NVMe-oF; foundational to AI/ML training infrastructure
notes: One of the most significant validated predictions in the paper; RDMA became a cornerstone of modern AI infrastructure.

metric_value: Freeing server CPU from TCP/IP processing via IBA router; stretches per-processor software licenses
notes: TOE prediction validated: TCP offload engines became commercial products (Chelsio Solarflare et al.).

metric_name: TCP offload engine commercialization — actual outcome
metric_value: TOE cards became commercial products 2004-2008; IBA TOE validated for HPC but commodity NICs eventually handled offload via kernel bypass
notes: iWARP and RoCE later standardized RDMA over Ethernet without dedicated TOE ASICs.

metric_name: Expected TCO reduction for database clusters
metric_value: ~50% TCO reduction; ~70% lower I/O management costs
notes: From field research with early-adopter enterprises evaluating IBA; not independently verified.

metric_value: Dozens of blades in rack create cabling rat's nest; server-to-server bandwidth and latency become performance limiters
notes: IBA switch in cabinet solves both: one IBA + one power cable per blade.

metric_name: Blades per rack in IBA reference design
metric_value: 12 processing blades with redundant switched backplane and common power
notes: Mellanox Technologies IBA blade server reference rack design; Figure 3 in original paper.

metric_value: PCI-X and PCI-X 2.0 are inadequate and will not satisfy long-term data center connectivity needs
notes: Aberdeen editorial judgment; 3GIO (later PCIe) also mentioned as serial PCI replacement starting 2004.

metric_value: PCI Express (PCIe) — not IBA — became the universal server I/O bus; IBA coexisted as an external fabric rather than replacing internal I/O
notes: IBA did NOT replace PCI-X in general enterprise as predicted; PCIe won the internal bus war.

metric_value: FC is an early network storage I/O protocol providing superior performance; IBA will route FC traffic and coexist initially
notes: IBA bridges to parallel SCSI and Fibre Channel during transition period.

metric_value: Large enterprise and research data centers where greatest need for expanded I/O bandwidth exists
notes: Accurate: HPC and research computing became the primary IBA domain.

metric_name: IBA HPC market share — actual outcome
metric_value: IBA >70% of TOP500 supercomputers by mid-2010s; dominant AI/ML training cluster fabric
notes: Matches Aberdeen's prediction of large enterprise/research as primary early domain; HPC proved to be the durable primary market.

metric_value: Compaq acquired by HP in 2002 during paper publication period
notes: Paper lists both Compaq and HP as IBTA steering committee members; merger completed mid-2002.

metric_value: Virtual Lanes allow QoS multiplexing on same physical link; every switch acts as QoS director
notes: VL-based QoS differentiation built into IBA spec; important for mixed storage/compute traffic.

metric_value: Decouples CPU from I/O controller; extends distance from inches to kilometers
notes: WAN storage placement enabled via IBA's global routing capability.

metric_value: iSCSI cited as example of storage-over-IP architecture trend aligned with IBA's fabric model
notes: iSCSI became widely adopted for mid-market SAN; complements rather than competes with IBA in different market segments.

metric_value: Enterprise production deployment possible starting 2003; first-gen HW/SW available late 2002
notes: Aberdeen recommended evaluation in 2002 and phased deployment in 2003.

metric_value: Leading manufacturers (Compaq/HP Dell IBM Unisys) to deliver servers with embedded HCA by 2003
notes: HCA plug-in PCI adapters in late 2002; silicon-integrated HCA in 2003 server products.

metric_name: Fabric topology as preferred storage networking architecture
metric_value: Aberdeen research identifies fabric topology as preferred architecture for storage networking
notes: Fibre Channel cited as existing fabric; IBA as next-generation fabric; iSCSI as IP-based fabric variant.

metric_value: Bandwidth scales as number of I/O ports increases; IBA overcomes shared bus bandwidth ceiling
notes: Key architectural advantage over PCI shared bus where total bandwidth is fixed regardless of device count.

metric_name: IBA as transport of choice for server clustering
metric_value: IBA well positioned to become transport of choice for inter-processor communications and server clustering
notes: Correct for HPC: IBA became the dominant MPI transport in TOP500 clusters.

metric_name: IBA influence on AI/ML training infrastructure
metric_value: IBA/Mellanox critical for NVIDIA AI/ML training clusters; NVIDIA acquired Mellanox for $6.9B in 2019-2020
notes: Ultimate validation of IBA's role in high-performance computing; NVIDIA acquisition driven by IBA's centrality to AI training.

metric_value: Multiple different cables in enterprise rack replaced with single common cable per server/storage unit
notes: Eliminates hundreds of cables; reduces I/O adapter count and overall costs.
